caps.reason: You cannot be placed abroad without a passport, so your readiness is held at needs prep until you have one in hand. It is the first thing to sort, and SA passport turnaround is usually a few weeks.

Every destination requires a completed trade test (Red Seal) before you can sit a skills assessment or be placed, so your readiness is held at needs prep until you have passed it. It is the single most important credential for going abroad as an artisan.

reason.Passed — certificate in hand: Red Seal trade test passed with the certificate on hand, the key credential every destination requires.
reason.Passed — certificate lost: Trade test passed but certificate lost, request a reprint from QCTO or your old SETA before applying.
reason.Booked / writing soon: Trade test booked but not yet passed, your overseas pathway opens once you have the result.
reason.Not yet — apprentice: Still an apprentice without a trade test, complete the Red Seal before pursuing offshore assessments.
reason.No formal trade test: No formal trade test, this is the primary blocker as every destination requires the Red Seal.

reason.Outcome received: Your TRA Offshore Skills Assessment outcome is in hand, the mandatory step for an Australian skilled visa.
reason.Approved by a province: Your Canadian provincial Trade Equivalency Assessment is approved, the key recognition step for Canada.
reason.Completed: Your New Zealand qualification check is complete, clearing the NZ recognition step.
reason.Submitted — in progress: Your destination skills assessment is underway; TRA runs about 15 weeks and Canadian provincial TEAs 3 to 12 months.
reason.Not started: No destination skills assessment started yet, the step that converts your Red Seal into a recognised overseas credential.

rules.empty_reason: No destination skills assessment recorded. Starting the right one for your target (TRA for Australia, a provincial TEA for Canada, or the NZ check) converts your Red Seal into a recognised overseas credential.

reason.Electrician: Electricians are in acute demand across Australia, Canada and the UK, one of the strongest trades to take abroad.
reason.Welder: Welders are a global shortage trade, expect strong hiring priority and good pay offshore.
reason.Boilermaker: Boilermakers are highly sought after in Australian mining and resources, very strong demand.
reason.Fitter & Turner: Fitters and turners are in steady demand across mining and manufacturing destinations.
reason.Diesel Mechanic: Diesel mechanics are in high demand in Australian and Canadian resource sectors, strong leverage.
reason.Millwright: Millwrights combine mechanical and electrical skills and are scarce worldwide, a significant advantage.
reason.Plumber: Plumbers are in solid demand abroad, though local licensing tests usually apply on arrival.
reason.Refrigeration / HVAC: Refrigeration and HVAC technicians are in good demand, especially in the Gulf and Australia.
reason.Auto Electrician: Auto electricians are sought after but the market is smaller than for core construction trades.
reason.Motor Mechanic: Motor mechanics have steady demand, target employers tied to skilled-visa sponsorship.
reason.Carpenter / Joiner: Carpenters and joiners are in moderate demand, strongest in Australia and New Zealand construction.
reason.Rigger: Riggers have moderate demand tied to mining and construction projects, target sponsoring employers.
reason.Bricklayer: Bricklayers see moderate demand, mainly in Australian and New Zealand residential construction.
reason.Painter: Painters face a smaller offshore market, pair your trade with strong references to stand out.
reason.Other: Confirm how your trade maps to the destination occupation list, as demand varies widely by trade.

rules.empty_reason: No trade recorded. High-demand trades such as Electrician, Welder, Boilermaker and Diesel Mechanic get hiring priority abroad, so add yours.

ranges.reason: Under 2 years post-qualification, most skilled-visa routes want more documented trade experience.
2 to 5 years post-qualification, meets the bar for most offshore assessments.
5 to 10 years post-qualification, a strong applicant for skilled-visa routes.
10+ years post-qualification, a senior tradesperson with deep documented experience.

reason.Yes — same trade: 12 recent months in your nominated trade, meeting the TRA recency requirement.
reason.Yes — but different/related trade: Recent work was in a related trade, document how it maps to your nominated occupation.
reason.No — gap of 1+ year: A gap of over a year in your trade may fail recency rules, get back into paid trade work.
reason.No — recently qualified: Recently qualified with limited recent hours, build up documented trade time before assessment.

reason.PAYE / IRP5 documented: Your work is PAYE/IRP5 documented, exactly what TRA and Canadian assessors accept as proof of experience.
reason.Self-employed (with SARS records): Self-employed with SARS records is assessable, so gather invoices, contracts and tax records to evidence your hours.
reason.Mix of PAYE and cash: A mix of PAYE and cash means only the documented portion counts toward an assessment, so maximise your evidenced hours.
reason.Other: Confirm how your work history can be evidenced, as assessors require documented, verifiable experience.
reason.Mostly cash-in-hand / family business: Cash-in-hand work without IRP5s or tax records is excluded from TRA and Canadian assessments, so you will need to build a documented track record.

reason.All employers contactable + letterhead r: Letterhead references from contactable employers meet the TRA OSAP and Canadian provincial requirements.
reason.Some employers contactable: Some employers are contactable, so secure letterhead references now while those contacts are still reachable.
reason.Old employers closed / unreachable: Unreachable past employers make references hard, so look for colleagues, payslips or contracts as alternative evidence.
reason.None: No employer references is a real obstacle, as every skills assessment requires verifiable letterhead references.

reason.Valid — 2+ years remaining: Passport valid with 2+ years remaining, meeting typical work-visa requirements.
reason.Valid — under 2 years remaining: Passport valid but renew soon, most work visas need 2+ years remaining.
reason.Expired: Passport expired, renew it before applying.
reason.No passport: No passport, apply immediately as this is the first blocker.

reason.Current (within 6 months): Current SAPS police clearance, meeting the visa-ready bar.
reason.Older than 6 months: Police clearance older than 6 months, most visa applications need one issued within 6 months.
reason.Applied / in progress: Police clearance applied for, time it to land near your visa lodgement.
reason.None: No police clearance, apply now as SAPS turnaround is typically 4 to 8 weeks.

reason.Under R50k: Below the typical R60k to R150k that trades relocation needs upfront, build savings first.
reason.R50k – R100k: Covers a lean solo relocation, tight once verification and assessment fees are included.
reason.R100k – R200k: A comfortable budget for verification, skills assessment, visa and flights.
reason.R200k – R400k: A strong financial position that covers relocation with dependants.
reason.R400k+: An excellent financial position for relocation with room for contingencies.
reason.Prefer not to say: Budget not declared, share it privately with us if it shapes your options.

reason.Native / first language: English as a first language clears the bar for every English-speaking destination.
reason.Fluent: Fluent English is sufficient for trades work in most destinations.
reason.Conversational: Conversational English works on site but skilled visas will want a test score.
reason.Basic: Basic English is a blocker for skilled-visa English tests, plan to improve before applying.

reason.IELTS Academic: IELTS Academic taken, note the UK Skilled Worker route needs IELTS for UKVI specifically.
reason.IELTS for UKVI (UK Skilled Worker): IELTS for UKVI taken, the correct test for the UK Skilled Worker route.
reason.IELTS General Training (Canada / NZ): IELTS General Training taken, accepted for Canada and New Zealand applications.
reason.PTE Academic: PTE Academic taken, accepted by Australia and several other destinations.
reason.None yet: No English test on record, skilled-visa routes will require one.

ranges.reason: No dependants, the easiest profile to relocate with no dependant visas needed.
One or two dependants, dependant visas add cost and paperwork but remain workable.
Three or more dependants, plan for higher visa costs, schooling and a larger relocation budget.

reason.As soon as possible: An ASAP timeline is realistic only once verification and assessment are already underway.
reason.Within 6 months: A 6-month horizon is tight given assessment timelines, start documents immediately.
reason.Within 1 year: A 1-year horizon suits the verification, skills assessment and visa sequence well.
reason.Within 2 years: A 2-year horizon gives ample time to verify documents and complete a skills assessment.
reason.Just exploring: Exploring only, no urgency means you can plan the sequence strategically.
